extraction.one_line_summary: MERLIER Tim won the bunch sprint after attacking long, while GANNA took defensive bonus seconds at intermediate sprint to protect maglia rosa.
tactical_patterns: Maglia rosa GANNA attacked intermediate sprint for bonus seconds, defending GC rather than conserving energy for team sprinter.
MERLIER went long into closing kilometer, exploiting lead-out confusion rather than waiting for optimal launch timing.
GAVIRIA forced wrong side of lead-out Milano when barriers held, showing sprint chaos creates barrier-side positioning gambles.

rider_signals.GANNA Filippo: positive: Attacking second intermediate sprint for bonus seconds shows Quick-Step prioritizing his GC defense over Viviani sprint setup
rider_signals.GROENEWEGEN Dylan: neutral: Fourth place impressive for first race in nine months but lacks top-end speed, suggesting form still building
rider_signals.GAVIRIA Fernando: negative: Had to navigate wrong side of own lead-out when barriers held, indicating positioning breakdown in final 200m
rider_signals.EWAN Caleb: negative: Never found position for 10th place, suggesting Trek lead-out train ineffective or absent on opening sprint stage
rider_signals.VIVIANI Elia: neutral: Glued to Gaviria's wheel tactically but couldn't accelerate past, revealing dependence on draft rather than independent positioning
